title: Washington Governor Signs Bill to Update the Stateâ€™s Clean Fuel Standard
content: On May 17, Washington Governor Bob Ferguson signed HB 1409 into law, substantially revising the stateâ€™s Clean Fuel Standard (CFS) by setting a new greenhouse gas (GHG) reduction target of 45% by 2038, relative to 2017 levels. Originally, the CFS, implemented in 2023, aimed for a 20% reduction by 2034. The new legislation accelerates and intensifies these targets, mandating a 5% additional reduction by 2026, 4% by 2027, and 3â€“4% annually through 2038. The Washington Department of Ecology may further increase the target to 55% post-2032 under certain conditions. However, the law also grants flexibility, allowing the agency to reduce targets in response to fuel supply constraints or cap the reduction at 20% from 2030 onward if no new or expanded biofuel facility in the state secures key permits after January 1, 2025. HB 1409 was introduced in January, passed by the House and Senate in March and April, respectively, and officially enacted in May 2025.
author: Analysts Account
